CREATE TABLE sample_data (id INTEGER, path VARCHAR);
SELECT id, parse_dirname(path, 'both_slash') AS top_level_dir FROM sample_data WHERE id = 1;
WITH path_data AS (SELECT path FROM sample_data WHERE id = 2) SELECT parse_dirname(path, 'forward_slash') FROM path_data;
SELECT parse_dirname(path, 'backslash') FROM sample_data WHERE id IN (1, 3);
CREATE TABLE other_data (type VARCHAR, path VARCHAR);
SELECT a.id, parse_dirname(a.path, 'system') AS dir_name, b.type FROM sample_data a JOIN other_data b ON a.path LIKE '%' || b.path || '%' WHERE b.type = 'video';
SELECT a.id, CASE WHEN parse_dirname(a.path, 'both_slash') = parse_dirname(b.path, 'both_slash') THEN 'MATCH' ELSE 'NO MATCH' END AS dir_comparison FROM sample_data a, other_data b;
SELECT COUNT(*), parse_dirname(path, 'backslash') AS top_dir FROM sample_data GROUP BY top_dir;
